channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.description:

item.title: Scientists Dig Up A 90-foot Giant Dinosaur In Thailand That Could Crush Four Elephants - The Daily Galaxy
item.description:
1. Scientists Dig Up A 90-foot Giant Dinosaur In Thailand That Could Crush Four Elephants  The Daily Galaxy

  4. Scientists identify massive new dinosaur following Thailand dig  Al Jazeera
  5. A new dinosaur dubbed the ‘Last Titan of Thailand’ weighed more than 9 elephants  Popular Science

item.title: NASA's Psyche spacecraft is flying to a metal asteroid. So, why did it just visit Mars? - Space
item.description:

item.title: First-ever direct image of the cosmic web reveals the Universe’s hidden highways - ScienceDaily
item.description:

item.title: Dark Matter May Have Been Detected by Accident, Scientists Reveal - ScienceAlert
item.description:

  5. Forty years and multi-tonne xenon detectors have brought dark-matter searches to the 'neutrino fog' without a signal, while a tentative hint surfaces in LIGO data built to listen for colliding black holes — and the pattern of paradigms exhausting themselves into a  Space Daily

item.title: What Mars Express just photographed in Shalbatana Vallis isn't just an ancient flood channel — it's the strongest topographic argument yet that Mars once held a real ocean - Space Daily

item.title: Voyager 1 is still transmitting from beyond the heliosphere on 22 watts — less power than the bulb in your hallway — and the engineers who built it in the 1970s never expected we'd still be listening half a century later. - Space Daily
item.description:

  3. Countdown: NASA’s race to keep the Voyager Probes “alive” for another year  CPG Click Petróleo e Gás
  4. NASA plans 2026 'Big bang' maneuver to preserve Voyagers's instruments  NewsBytes
  5. NASA’s Voyager probes are defying time, and this bold plan could extend their mission for decades  MSN
